observations.recommendation:
1.6	We recommended the following to the Management:
The Inventory Committee to undertake the physical inventory of PPE and conduct the one-time cleansing;
To conduct a complete inventory count of all PPEs of the LGU and submit a Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; and
To reconcile the inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and undertake all other procedures prescribed under the same Circular to come up with the correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

2.6	We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the related expenditures from the PPE account to the appropriate CIP account;
Recognize depreciation expenses in the period the assets become available for use, in accordance with Paragraph 71 of IPSAS 17, to reflect accurate balances in the Statement of Financial Position and Statement of Financial Performance; and
Ensure that all expenditures related to construction of projects are initially recorded as CIP and reclassify all completed projects from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE account immediately upon project completion, as required under Section 50, Chapter 3 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.

3.8	We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of P.D. 1445.
3.10 The Audit Team further recommended to investigate and exhaust all possible remedies to determine the causes of the lacking records and to reconcile these accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
Review and reconcile all transactions related to inter-local government unit transactions to ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entries to reflect the correct account balances.

5.11	We recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.
5.12	We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan, including continuing appropriations, and closely coordinate with the concerned departments in the implementation of these projects, in order for the Municipality to fully achieve the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes.

We recommended that the Management revert all savings from completed projects to the unappropriated surplus, which shall be available for use for other expenditures or priority development projects of the LGU.

7.7	We recommended that the Management transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book.

8.5	We recommended that the Management:
Immediately revert the unexpended LDRRMF balance of P584,556.05 from CY 2018 to the General Fund; and
Implement a monitoring system to track the utilization of LDRRMF and ensure compliance with R.A No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the five-year reversion rule.

9.9	We recommended that the Management follow the specific guidelines provided for Direct Retail Purchase of petroleum fuels, oils and lubricants in Section 53.14 and Annex H of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, specifically the inclusion of estimate POL products in the Annual Procurement Plan and execution of contract/agreement between the Municipality and the supplier to warrant any damage/loss.

10.7	We recommended that the Management:
Adopt policies and internal control measures to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of an employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of P5,000.00; and
Direct those in-charge of payroll preparation to exercise diligence in monitoring net take home pay of personnel to comply with the requirement of existing laws.

We recommended that Management require the:
a. Inventory Committee to continuously make utmost effort to conduct a
complete and detailed physical inventory of all the LGU’s properties in
accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular
No. 2020-006;
b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs) for all PPEs; and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make a final
reconciliation of their respective records and avail the one-time cleansing of
PPE account balances, including the disposition and derecognition of non-
existing/missing PPE and those without available records of accountability
and adjust the books accordingly, in order to come up with reliable PPE
balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer conduct a thorough review of the unverified/unreconciled deposits and
withdrawals from prior years and submit the revised BRS along with the proof, such as
deposit slips and details of these book and bank reconciling items to the Office of the
Auditor.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strengthen accounting
controls over the Cash in Bank account, and effect necessary adjustments for valid
reconciling items, including the outstanding checks which could be considered stale.

We recommended that Management strengthen internal control over collections,
remittances and deposits to avoid retention of amounts of money in the hands of the AO
and consider the promulgation and implementation of a policy on the following:
a. Verification by the Municipal Treasurer of the unused accountable forms in
the possession of collecting clerks upon remittance to ensure that all
collections are remitted daily and intact;
b. Limitations in the issuance of receipts to the collectors, including the non-
issuance of new stubs of receipts to those with unremitted collections; and
c. Strict adherence to the guidelines relative to the remittance and deposits of
collections prescribed under Sections 29 and 32 of Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that Management require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all inventory items
in their possession. Likewise, the Property/Supply Officer must prepare and
submit a duly accomplished RPCI;
b. Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of inventories for
consumption or distribution in the appropriate inventory accounts and the
consumption or distribution thereof as expense, upon receipt of SSMI of a
given period; and
c. Accounting Office and the Treasury Office to maintain SLCs and SCs,
respectively, and to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and make
adjustments, when necessary, to reflect the appropriate balance of the
Inventory Accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management:
a. Exhaust all possible means to require the concerned officers and employees to
liquidate/settle immediately their outstanding CAs by serving demand letters to
AOs, not only to those still connected with the LGU but also to those who
retired, resigned and transferred to other office/s, to immediately submit their
liquidation papers or settle their accountabilities with the LGU; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of
outstanding CAs, refrain from granting additional CAs to those who have
unliquidated CAs, and enforce the use of Certification that previous cash
advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books as part of the
documentary requirements.

We recommended that Management:
a. Fast-track the implementation of projects sourced from NGAs, as the funds
are readily available, and this will enable the benefits of the project to be
enjoyed by the constituents at the earliest possible time; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to review all entries and transactions
made for the fund transfers, specifically those that were fully implemented
but still outstanding, as well as those unverified balances without detailed
documentation. This review must include the previous years’ records and
drawing of correcting entries to arrive at the accurate balances to be
presented in the FS. If the outstanding balances in the Due to NGAs account
are determined to be funds for completed projects and the remaining
balances are no longer needed, we recommended that Management remit
them back to the source agencies as required by existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Analyze the affected accounts and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries
to reflect the correct balances of each account in their respective fund or special
accounts classification; and
b. Examine prior transactions affecting the reciprocal accounts, specifically on
the unverified balances in the Due from Other Funds of the GF and Due to
Other Funds of the TF amounting to ₱5,580.00 and ₱110,788.80, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare a JEV to rectify the erroneous adjusting entries in the CIP-Buildings and Other
Structures account of the TF including the subsequent transfer to the Buildings account
in the GF. Henceforth, reclassify CIP accounts to the appropriate asset account only
upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to install control measures on the observance of the required timelines in the
submission of accounts/reports to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, require the
Office of the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls
accounts and issued ORs on or before the 5th day of the ensuing month, to the
Accounting Office for recording in the Agency books of accounts, after which, the same
must be submitted to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month including
their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular
No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management:
a. Exert more efforts to maximize the implementation of development projects,
charged against the 20 percent DF for CY 2024 and continuing
appropriations, to ensure that social, economic, and environmental outcomes
essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality
of life for the LGU’s constituents are achieved; and
b. Meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before their
inclusion in the DF and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and provide realistic
timelines for their completion.

We recommended that Management:
a. Enjoin the LDRRMO to exert more effort in the implementation of PPAs
under the LDRRM to optimize its utilization and to help build a disaster-
resilient LGU and communities;
b. Instruct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit in a prescribed format the
monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF with complete
and sufficient information on time and facilitate monitoring of the
63
accomplishment vis-à-vis the targeted projects for effective and efficient
utilization of the fund;
c. Direct the LDRRMO to prepare the annual LDRRMFIP detailing all the
PPAs to be funded, including the list of PPAs to be undertaken out of the
previous years’ unexpended balances under STF using the prescribed Format
in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate RAAOs for LDRRMF
in compliance with Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate
proper control and monitoring of appropriations, allotments and obligations
under the said fund.

We recommended that Management:
a. Exert more efforts in the implementation of PPAs embodied in the GPB to
ensure the full attainment of all the envisioned GAD objectives of the LGU;
b. Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to
fast-track the gathering of data and information needed for the creation of an
updated and well-established GAD database to have an accurate and reliable
basis in the planning and programming of PPAs as well as in the policy
formulation in order to properly address the existing and emerging GAD
issues and concerns;
c. Direct the GFPS and GAD key personnel to undergo capacity building
programs and training on gender and development to enhance their
knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and
international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-
responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools like Harmonized
Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) on the attributed budget to
evaluate gender responsiveness of PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget; and
d. Require the GFPS to update the GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines
prescribed by PCW.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the concerned officers to prepare the prescribed Quarterly Report on
Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation of LGSF-
SBDP and thereafter post the same in at least three conspicuous places within
the locality;
b. Likewise, post the aforementioned quarterly reports on the LGU’s website
and notify in writing the NTF-ELCAC Vice Chairperson and Regional Task
Force (RTF)-ELCAC concerned, Speaker of the House of Representatives,
President of the Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House
70
Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance when the
reports are already posted thereon; and
c. Submit the printout copies or any proof of posting of NOA, NTP and contract
in the PhilGEPS, LGU’s website and any conspicuous places.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to submit the
required lacking supporting documents and DVs and ensure that payments for progress
and final billings were properly supported with complete documentation in accordance
with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to strictly comply with the
provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and Section 272 of RA No. 7160, such
as to:
a. Formulate a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization policies in
the allocation of the SEF to schools;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit to
the LSB, not later than July 15 of the current year, the actual SEF income of
the preceding periods and the estimated income of the succeeding periods to
appropriately project a realistic SET collectible in the ensuing year;
c. Prepare a SEF Budget of the ensuing year not later than October 31 of the
current year, ensuring that the PPAs for ECCD be incorporated in this Budget
and incorporating thereof the SEF balance per bank statements amounting to
₱5,894,420.71 as of December 31, 2024; and
d. Submit the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization using the
prescribed form, prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd Central
Office and COA within the prescribed period.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management is amenable to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Make necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the Land and Interest Payable accounts by reversing the original entries made in the amount of ₱58.257 million; and
Ensure that proper accounting procedures are followed for all future loan transactions, including the correct treatment of interest expenses, liabilities, and/or capitalization to a qualifying asset account by complying with IPSAS and other relevant accounting standards, and referring to the Audit Team, if necessary.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the processing and grant of Cash Advances for Payment of Wages without the approved payrolls or lists of payees indicating their net payments duly attached to the disbursement vouchers;
Instruct the Heads of Offices and the employees to strictly implement and religiously comply with the Municipality’s policy on payments of wages, to the effect that no wages shall be released without the submission of DTRs and ARs which are duly signed by the head of office and the employee; and
In coordination with the HRMO, revisit the Municipality’s schedule on release of wages to the employees and consider providing enough processing time after every payroll cut-off date to allow accurate computation of wages due, including the necessary deductions, and ensure completeness of supporting documents before recommending the release of payment of salaries/wages.

Recommendations:
3.15 We recommended and Management concurred to instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the improper recognition of wages expenses at a gross amount which includes specifically the deductions for absences, lateness and/or undertime. It is also recommended that the adjustment thereof be effected in the books of accounts.

Recommendations:
4.13 We recommended and it was agreed by Management to perform the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
Ensure that upon release of checks, check registers are duly signed by the intended recipients and the date of receipt is indicated;
Submit the monthly reports of collections and/or disbursements and other required reports to the Municipal Accountant within the prescribed period.
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Submit the BRS within the prescribed period and ensure that reconciling items are identified properly and adjusted accordingly and promptly;
Follow up with the bank the retrieval of data relative to the unidentified credit memos under LBP Account No. 3532-xxxx-09.
Evaluate the current manning composition of the Accounting and Treasury Office and other concerned operating units, and consider possible qualified additions to the manpower specifically the personnel in charge of the preparation of BRS as well as the submission of reports and documents necessary for the prompt preparation thereof, respectively;
Issue a memorandum to all concerned offices / operating units requiring prompt submission of reports and documents necessary in the preparation of the BRS and other related reports; and
4.13.5    Consider enrolling the rest of the bank accounts in online banking to facilitate easy and prompt access and monitoring of account transactions.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
12.9.1  Instruct the Municipal Tourism Officer to coordinate closely with the Human Resource Management Officer as to the proper hiring process of contractual personnel pursuant to pertinent laws, rules and regulations, and ensure that proper documentation is provided, such as, but not limited to signed contracts/appointments, approved TORs, and supporting documents for each contractual hire; and
Submit explanation and/or justification on the validity, regularity, and legality of payments made to various trainers, dancers, and other artists totaling ₱2.402 million, considering the lack of necessary documents and data to support any claims to the contrary.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
15.9.1 Instruct the BAC to immediately submit the above contracts and other supporting documents, and ensure thereafter the submission of contracts and all other required attachments to COA for review and evaluation within the prescribed period;
Define the specific roles and responsibilities of the BAC Secretariat, the end-users and other concerned employee/staff in the process of collating and submitting procurement documents; and
Assess the personnel component of the BAC Secretariat and make appropriate measures in the manner of delegation of tasks to concerned personnel to address the delay in the submission of contracts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical count of all inventory items semestrally and render a report thereon within the prescribed period;

observations.managementAction:
There is difficulty in obtaining supporting documents.

The supporting documents and records can no longer be traced.

observations.recommendation:

Direct the Inventory Committee and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the total road network in the Notes to the FS in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Lack of manpower and difficulty in obtaining supporting documents especially for prior year road projects

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to exert more effort in preparing and submitting the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting documents and schedules, within the prescribed period. We requested that such activities be given utmost priority for timely and better fiscal reporting.
observations.managementAction: The Management acknowledges the importance of prioritization of timely submission of all financial reports to promote better timely reporting, Hence, it continuously formulates ways and strategies to meet the deadlines for the submission of accounts and financial reports without prejudice to the existing laws and circulars.
observations.reason: The weather has been an important consideration in complying the audit recommendation due to overseas conveyance of documents. Also, incomplete documents in the preparation of vouchers impeded in the speedy submission of the accounts.

observations.recommendation:

Maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the programs, projects and activities within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all;

Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the program, project and activity, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and

Adopt strategies to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of reported accomplishments to guarantee to the stakeholders the accuracy and reliability of information that are relevant in decision-making.

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to spearhead the setting up and maintenance of GAD database to serve as basis in gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The Management already directed the concerned personnel to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and difficulty in finding bidders for GAD projects

observations.recommendation:

Unavailability of prior years’ records.

Lack of manpower

In-depth evaluation to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables both for the current and prior year’s ledgers/files to determine which are valid claims or fictitious accounts; and

The Office of the Municipal Accountant to take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for those established transactions with no valid claimants and with dormant accounts in order to have a fair presentation of accounts in the FS.

Inability to provide the necessary documents to support all accounts payable.

Ongoing retrieval of prior years’ records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require strict adherence to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Secretariat has poor internal control on its record management and lack of coordination with the technical personnel resulting to incomplete/non-compliance of the needed documentation of the PPAs implemented by the agency.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant monitored the completeness of documentations pertaining to the project implementation disbursements.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat has poor internal control on its record management and lack of coordination with the technical personnel resulting to incomplete/non-compliance of the needed documentation of the PPAs implemented by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all insurable properties be insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS in the ensuing year and onwards as well as see to it that monitoring measures are in place in the renewal before its due date. Ensure that funds are allocated to cover the premiums for the insurance so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated for any damage or losses arising from the risks the properties were insured for.
observations.managementAction: No funds were allocated for the payment of insurance.
observations.reason: No funds were allocated for the payment of insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense, prepare the lapsing schedule and draw the necessary journal entry to take up depreciation expense for the current year, update the related accumulated depreciation and record adjustment in government equity account for unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expense to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 17 and pertinent provisions of Item IV of  COA Circular No.2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable information pursuant to Section 111(2) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Management timely implement the funded programs or projects by the National Government in order to maximize the use of government resources and prompt delivery of the desired socio- economic outcomes to its constituents.
We also recommended that the Management immediately prepare the corresponding liquidation documents to be submitted to the source agency and return the unutilized balances of the fund in accordance with Item 6.7 of COA Circular.

We recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan, including continuing appropriations, and closely coordinate with the concerned departments in the implementation of these projects, in order for the Municipality to fully achieve the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes.

We recommended the following:
The Management to submit the Acknowledgement Receipts duly signed by the farmer beneficiaries, indicating the quantity and specific agricultural supplies received by them;
The Management to account for the remaining undistributed farm inputs and require the supplier to deliver the farm inputs to the Municipality, and assume the responsibility of distributing the farm inputs to the farmers; and
The Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of supplies and materials in accordance with the perpetual inventory method as prescribed in Section 114 of Volume 1 of NGAS for LGUs and effect the necessary correcting journal entries.

We recommended the following:
The Management submit justification on the payment of CNA Incentive despite non-compliance with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations;
The Management submit the following necessary documents to satisfy the requirements for the grant of CNA as follows:
Resolution signed by both parties incorporating the guidelines/criteria for granting CNA Incentive;
Certificate issued by the Head of the Agency on the total amount of unencumbered savings generated from cost-cutting measures identified in the CNA which resulted from the joint efforts of labor and management and systems/productivity/income improvement; and
Proof that the planned programs/activities/projects have been implemented and completed in accordance with targets for the year; and
The Management ensure compliance with the conditions and guidelines, and documentary requirements before the release/payment of CNA Incentive.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that Management:
Exert continued efforts in completing the physical count and in coming up with complete and accurate RPCPPE and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in relation to the deficiencies noted; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer together with the Inventory Committee to intensify the review and reconciliation of their records on PPE to come up with reconciled balance of these accounts and direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain Property Cards and PPELCs of all PPE, respectively, for proper identification of the PPE of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit the duly certified list of RPT collectible for the year to the Accounting Office in order to establish the RPT/SET Receivables accounts at the beginning of the year in compliance with Sec. 20 of the Manual of NGAS for LGUs; and
Require the Municipal Assessor to prepare an accurate assessment roll on the real properties of the Municipality which serves as the basis for the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Agency’s Inventory Committee/Appraisal Committee in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to verify the existence and ascertain the fair value less cost to sell of the identified Biological Assets, and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Accounting Office;
Direct the Municipal Accountant, based on the report to be prepared by the concerned personnel, to prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Breeding Stock account.  Henceforth, Biological Assets, at each reporting date, must be measured at fair value less cost to sell as required in IPSAS 27; and
Advise the Municipal Agriculturist to regularly furnish the report on the changes in the fair value of the Biological Assets of the LGU to ensure up-to-date fair value measurement of the Biological Assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections and not to allow delayed and partial remittances from collectors as required by rules and regulations to prevent possible misappropriation, theft, and loss of funds.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and any items subject for correction must be adjusted promptly to come up with the correct balance of the said account to be presented in the FS.

We recommended that Management:
Communicate in writing to the source agency to seek their decision on the disposition of the unutilized/remaining fund/s; and
Require the concerned offices to fast track the implementation of the intended programs/projects of the aforementioned funds.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Require the concerned local officials to submit the required reports namely: supplemental LDIP and AIP and Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation in accordance with DBM LBC No.151;
b.	Submit the lacking documentary requirements and explanation of the noted deficiencies; and
c.	Henceforth, submit contract agreements within five working days after its perfection and ensure that the submitted DVs are supported with complete documentary requirements.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that all disbursements made by the Municipality are supported with complete documentation as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-001;
Ensure that the attached documents, such as the Purchase Orders, IARs, etc. are completely and properly signed and accomplished in compliance with the applicable laws and regulations to ascertain the validity and propriety of every transaction made by the Municipality; and
Submit the lacking documentary requirements and explanation of the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned officials to exert more effort in preparing and submitting the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting documents and schedules, within the prescribed period; and
Require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to install control measures on the observance of the required timelines in the submission of accounts/reports to the Office of the Auditor. Further, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for recording in the Agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly enforce the submission of reports on information and publicity of LGU PPAs as provided in COA Circular No.  2013-004; and
Direct the Project Engineers/Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 2.2.6 of COA Circular 2013-004 and avoid display of any color, symbol, or graphic representation which are deemed unnecessary.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that Management:
Maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic, and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all;
Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the PPAs and their corresponding timelines, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and
Adopt strategies to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of reported accomplishments to guarantee the stakeholders of the accuracy and reliability of information in their decision-making.

We recommended that the Management require the BAC to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MDRRM Officer - Designate and the Human Resource Management (HRM) Officer to initiate the designation of additional two positions in the LDRRMO with appropriate training to properly institutionalize the organization of LDRRMO as prescribed under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended that the LCE through the Solid Waste Management Board to:
Exert effort in collecting data on the Municipality’s waste diversion rate and waste disposal rate incorporated in its 10-year SWMP to evaluate whether or not it was able to achieve its goal;
Encourage active participation of Barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality especially the segregation of waste at source; and
Monitor the establishment of MRF of each barangay.

We recommended that Management require the following:
The Accounting Unit to coordinate with the MTO and immediately submit to COA the duplicate copies of the ORs for CYs 2022 to 2023, and thereafter ensure submission thereof as attachment to the RCDs; and
The MTO to retain only the triplicate copies of the ORs and refrain from retaining the duplicate copies thereof.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly require all outgoing AOs to prepare the following documents upon turn-over, when applicable, and submit them immediately to the Auditor’s Office:
Duly certified Cut-off Report of Cash and/or Property Accountability
•	For Collecting Officers- type of collections should be itemized and proof of deposit of any remaining balance attached therewith
Invoice Receipt for Property– for the inventory of official documents and unused accountable forms to be turned over to the incoming AO which should be duly certified and acknowledged by the outgoing and incoming AOs, respectively.
Discontinue the practice of transferring accountability from one officer to another without prior notice to COA pursuant to Section 75 of P.D. No. 1445, and ensure thereafter that a notice together with the report of the remaining accountability is officially communicated to COA so that the outgoing AOs will be subjected to cash examination to establish their accountabilities at the time they cease to act as AOs or upon transfer of office.

We recommended that management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Certified List of Taxpayers showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, the amount of which shall be computed based on the Taxpayers’ Index prepared by the Municipal Assessor and the outstanding balances from the prior year’s receivables; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Refer to the above certified list as basis in the preparation of journal entry to set-up the RPT and SET receivables for the year and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income; and
Review the discrepancy between the RPT and SET receivables and take up necessary adjustments thereon.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
In coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, carefully scrutinize and update the certified list of RPT Tax Delinquencies and provide aging thereof for proper monitoring;
Formulate strategies in intensifying tax collection such as but not limited to:
In order to determine the actual owner and beneficial users of the real properties:
a.i  Strengthen the linkages with other officials and agencies such as the Register of Deeds and real estate dealers who are sources of property transaction information;
a.ii Seek assistance from the Barangay officials and employees.
Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and coordinate with the Provincial Government of Misamis Oriental to enforce administrative and judicial remedies as provided in the LGC; and
Employ other methods to encourage the payment of tax delinquencies.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GSO to prepare and submit semestral inventory report using the prescribed RCPI form and fill in all the data required therein, furnishing the Auditor a copy thereof;
Instruct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare / maintain Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, both per stock number, respectively, and conduct regular reconciliation of their respective records / reports on Inventories; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Record all purchases of inventoriable supplies under the appropriate Inventory Account; and
Require the GSO to consolidate weekly all Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit monthly to the Accounting Office such report together with the approved RIS to support the recording of issuances as expense pursuant to Section 121 of NGAS Manual Vol. 1.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting detail/schedules and to provide adequate and necessary disclosures in the Notes to the FS pursuant to the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
Assess the composition of the BAC Secretariat staff whether it is sufficient to cater to the demands of the Office, and based on the results of the assessment, make necessary adjustments in the delegation of tasks to concerned personnel to address the delay in submission of contracts, if necessary; and
Instruct the BAC to immediately submit the above contracts and other supporting documents, and ensure thereafter the submission of contracts and all other required attachments to COA for review and evaluation within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books, and ensure thereafter the regular review of pertinent transactions and recording thereof in the books to deter any errors and omissions.

We recommended that Management require the Inspection Team/Committee to inform COA about deliveries of procured items and furnish copies of the IARs within 24 hours after the acceptance by the personnel concerned so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Make necessary adjustments in the books for the total borrowing costs incurred in CY 2023 by debiting the appropriate asset account and crediting the Prior Period Adjustment account in the amount of P1.683 million; and
Ensure that borrowing costs capitalized during the period are presented and disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation of the balances recorded under the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books and related records to ensure that all contributions and loan payments withheld are properly accounted for and remitted accordingly on a timely manner to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances as well as the imposition of unnecessary penalties / surcharges by the concerned agencies.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation of the balances recorded under the Due to BIR accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books and related records / BIR forms to ensure that all taxes withheld are properly accounted for and remitted accordingly on a timely manner and to avoid unnecessary penalties / surcharges.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Identify the various projects in all the Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts from the accounting records. Compare this to the listing of projects by year obtained from the office of Municipal Engineering;
Maintain Construction in Progress Ledger Cards (CIPLC) for all projects/assets constructed in accordance with the Government Accounting Manual, Volume II; and
Effect the necessary adjusting entry for the transfer of the costs of the completed project from the CIP accounts to Infrastructure Assets and Building and Other Structure Accounts, recording in the Registry of Public Infrastructure and ensure that in recording transactions, the proper accounts are used.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with and submit to the Municipal Accountant a list of all projects undertaken by year with all the needed information for reconciliation purposes and to assist in facilitating/gathering the documents required to adjust the CIP accounts.

We recommended that management require the:
GSO to enforce the utilization of Inventory Custodian Slips and Requisition Issue Slips in issuing supplies and inventories to end-users and regularly update its stock cards for monitoring, recording and reporting purposes;
Municipal Accountant to strictly require the submission of monthly SSMI supported with approved RIS from the GSO so as to support of the recording of issuances as expense pursuant to Section 121 of NGAS Manual Vol. 1; and
Conduct of the physical count of inventory items by type every semester and submit the required Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the auditor.

We recommended that management:
Direct the municipal treasurer to require the real property taxpayers to pay the under-collection, otherwise she will be held personally liable for total amount uncollected; and
Direct the municipal treasurer to instruct her collectors to strictly comply with the provisions of the Local Government Code and BLGF-DOF Local Treasury Operations Manual on granting discounts to real property taxpayers.

We recommended the management to:
a. 	Strictly comply with the Sec. 63 and 68 of Presidential Decree 1445 in accounting for all moneys officially received by its officials and employees in any capacity or upon any occasion as government funds through proper recording and remittance of collections and expenses to the Municipal Treasurer and Accounting;
b. 	Secure an approved municipal ordinance from the local sanggunian for the establishment of the View deck as a Local Economic Enterprise upon compliance with the requirements of DBM LBC No. 111; and
c. 	Discontinue the practice of use of public properties for private purposes in compliance with Sec. 4(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical count of all inventory items semestrally and render a report thereon within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12 and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain and update the stock cards and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.

We recommended that Management:
Require all the concerned AOs to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately by demanding the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents. Otherwise, cause the withholding or suspension of their salaries for non-compliance therewith;
Strengthen the control measures to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash advances by strictly monitoring the period of liquidation and impose sanctions on AOs who are unable to liquidate on time; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of RPT and SET collected in CY 2021. Likewise, she must follow the correct accounting entries regarding collections of RPT and SET in order to reflect the correct amount of RPT and SET income, receivables and other affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Officer in Charge of the CWS and CES must furnish the Municipal Accountant of the Monthly Billing, Reading, and Payment Monitoring Report for basis of the latter in recording actual income earned and its corresponding receivable. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the accrual basis of accounting in accordance with IPSAS 1.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Trace and locate the relevant documents to serve as basis in analyzing the reconciling items and prepare adjustments, if any; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the discrepancy.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to exert extra efforts in the retrieval of land titles and other necessary documents as basis to establish ownership and determine proper valuation;
Allocate adequate budget for the titling of the subject land properties in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and reduce the risk of third party claims; and
Conduct proper evaluation of the two RPUs to facilitate the immediate recognition as assets of the Municipality considering the criteria provided under IPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management prioritize the creation of the remaining three plantilla positions for LDRRMO in accordance with the provisions set forth in RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 so that efforts will be coordinated in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building disaster-resilient local communities.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB must strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.
Likewise, we recommended that the LSB strictly comply with the requirements of the said JC in identifying the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenses and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which it was established.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to:

Submit immediately the subject Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents. Thereafter, coordinate with the accountable officers and all concerned to submit promptly or timely the disbursement reports together with the paid vouchers, payrolls and its supporting documents; and 

Suspend the payment of the salaries of the officials responsible for the non-submission of the subject reports until they shall have complied with the requirements.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to:

a.Strictly adhere to Section 80 of the GAM for LGUs and other relevant provisions on the cash payment to laborers through the disbursing officer and other authorized personnel; and

b. Discontinue the payment of check to one of the laborers and have them all sign in the payroll as proof of the receipt of the amount of salary/compensation.

We recommended that management:

a. Strictly adhere to the provisions of EO 77 and other provisions on the grant of travel expenses claims.
Discontinue the grant of excessive amount of travel claims based on the guidelines and stop the approval of unnecessary travels. 

Refund the excessive claims and unnecessary travel expenses.

We recommended that management:

a) Strictly adhere to the provisions of PD 1445 to attach the complete documentation for claims against government funds;

b) Observe strictly the provisions of RA 9184 and other relevant pronouncements in the conduct of the procurements and to hold an open and competitive bidding process in the procurement of seedlings and other similar goods; and

c)Recognize and reflect the proper accounting entries for the transactions and present fairly the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management:

a. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001, DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-05 and other relevant provisions in the grant of Collective Negotiation Agreement benefits; 

b.Discontinue the grant of CNA benefits without complete and proper documentation; and

c.Submit the necessary documentation as proof of the propriety and validity of the benefits granted.

We recommended the Management to:

Meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process and that the BAC and its members to adhere strictly with the Revised IRR of RA 9184, particularly, Sections 48.2, 52.1 and 54 regarding the conduct of public bidding, posting in the Philgeps and adopting the alternative mode of procurement in the acquisition of goods and services in CY 2020 and the years thereafter; and

Attach all the required supporting documents, particularly the SEC registration and Business Permits of the catering services providers, showing the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended that management:

Strictly adheres to Items No 1 and 6, Section 4 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 to support with appropriation law and complete documentation particularly the grant of allowances to officials and employees.

Discontinue the grant of allowances to job order personnel other than what is stipulated in the contract and refund the communication allowance received.

Attach a duly approved Sangguniang Bayan resolution or legal basis in the grant of communication allowance to officials and employees.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the BAC Secretariat to comply with the mandatory posting of procurement information for contracts awarded through Emergency Procurement.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense, prepare the lapsing schedule and draw the necessary journal entry to take up depreciation expense for the current year, update the related accumulated depreciation and record the adjustment in the government equity account for unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expense to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with PPSAS 17 and pertinent provisions of Item IV of  COA Circular No.2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the Accumulated Impairment Losses-Investment Property-Markets to Accumulated Depreciation-Investment Property-Markets to conform with PPSAS 16 and COA Circular No. 2015-002, and for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Officer monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan, including continuing appropriations, and closely coordinate with the concerned departments in the implementation of these projects, in order for the Municipality to fully achieve the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, ensure that the unexpended balances of DRRMF-MOOE be transferred to the Special Trust Fund account and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that only the capital outlay component of the DRRMF should be treated as Continuing Appropriations.

We recommended that the Management submit a justification on the payment of Honoraria to the assigned Municipal Local Government Operations Officer, otherwise, discontinue the payment of honoraria and other allowances without legal basis.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit/remit the balance of ₱184,834 in the ensuing year.  

Also, require all Accountable Officers (AOs) to desist from handling huge amount of cash in their possession and deposit all their collections in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS, thus, discouraging them from using their collections for personal or other purposes.

We recommended that Management require all AOs to settle their respective cash advances at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the books of accounts of the Agency.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
a. Require the Accounting Office to trace out the records pertaining to the CIP accounts without the supporting schedules and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in securing the copies of accomplishment report on the implemented projects on previous years;
b. Prepare and maintain CIPLC for each on-going infrastructure projects; and
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer whose powers and duties requires her to take custody of and be accountable for all properties real or personal owned by the Agency, to secure the copy of the Monthly Status Report of Projects Undertaken and validate the existence of the ongoing infrastructure projects to attest to the correctness of the accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The  Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents of all accumulated AP as reflected in the Schedule of AP as of December 31, 2019 to back up the authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government;
In-depth evaluation to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables both for the current and prior year’s ledgers/files to determine which are valid claims or fictitious accounts; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for those established transactions with no valid claimants and with dormant accounts in order to have a fair presentation of accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the respective amount payable to the 20 barangays of the Municipal Government as well as to the Province of Masbate as mandated under the existing regulations.
Further, require the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to the prompt release of the barangay shares from realty taxes as stated under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code and shares from CTCs collections in the ensuing years.

We recommended that Management design and implement real property tax generation program which must include tax mapping, complete and updated taxpayers records and strategies to improve tax collection efficiency especially on delinquent account.  Send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and avail the aforementioned legal remedies.  Moreover, inculcate to taxpayers, thru information dissemination, that taxes collected were applied to address the most urgent needs of the general public.

We recommended that Management direct the Officials concerned to ensure that 20 per cent of the Internal Revenue Allotment is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development. Strict observance of the timetable for the implementation of specific phase of activities to be undertaken will aid the Agency in prioritizing activities to be implemented given the available resources for that period to achieve results.
Implement immediately the programs and projects relative to the unutilized balance of ₱19.69 million as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management provide the Audit Team with a copy of the LDRRMC Resolution together with the report on the assessment of the extent of damage or losses suffered from the calamity and justification why the QRF was released by way of cash advance to an elected official. observations.reason: The GAD Focal Point System of the municipality is not strengthened which resulted in poor monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs if it had addressed the identified gender issues/concerns in the locality. Further, we have noted the delayed submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report thus, precluded the Audit Team from conducting verification. Moreover, we have noted that out of the allocated amount ₱6,807,728.60 for the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities, ₱4,425,232.40 or 65% of which were utilized and ₱2,382,496.20 were not utilized as of year end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to evaluate the Local Road Inventory Report and reconcile with the accounting records to facilitate the reporting of the municipality’s road network system;
Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and thereafter,
Municipal Accountant to record amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.

We recommended management, thru the Accounting Office, to reclassify the Markets account to Investment Property, Building account to conform to IPSAS 16-Investment Property for fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that management notify and direct all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the receipt and inspection of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end user.  The GSO shall likewise furnish the accounting department the necessary documents on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts.  The accounting office shall make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to effect the corrections on unrecorded transactions pursuant to IPSAS 23.

We recommended that Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and fully deliver on time these benefits to the community.
Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the MDRRM Council formulate a Comprehensive and Integrated MDRRM Plan.  The MDRRM Office is advised to closely monitor the MDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/ activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risks and enhancing disaster preparedness capabilities at LGU level.

We recommended that Management through the MSWMB fully execute the programs and activities contained in the ESWMP, make a periodic review of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, undertake self-assessment report and make corrective action plan to address the present issues and concerns on ecological environment.

We recommended that Management through the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 in the implementation of GAD Plan. To fully achieve the targeted objectives of the GAD Plan, the budget intended should be fully utilized during the year to avoid reversion of funds. Likewise, periodic review and corrections should be made on GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women including other gender related issues.

We recommended that Management formulate Policy Guidelines in hiring contract of service employees, taking into consideration the necessity as to the reasonable number of contract of service employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and unnecessary expenditures for wages.
Ensure that the policy guidelines formulated would not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality but promote optimum utilization of financial resources.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit/remit the balance of ₱196,065 in the ensuing year.  Also, require all Accountable Officers to desist from handling huge amount of cash in their possession and deposit all their collections in accordance with the NGAS, thus, discouraging them from using their collections for personal or other purposes.

We recommended that Management enjoin all Accountable Officers to settle their respective cash advances at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the books of accounts of the Agency.  Henceforth, avoid granting subsequent cash advances to Accountable Officers with outstanding cash advances and withhold salaries and/or any amount due those Accountable Officers who were unable to comply with pertinent rules and regulations on cash advances.  Adhere strictly to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances as enunciated in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Further, cause for the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱5.88 million as of December 31, 2018 and submit the liquidation report to the Auditor for post-audit. Send Demand Letters to the concerned Accountable Officer(s) with unliquidated cash advances for monitoring purposes.

We recommended the following to Management:
1) Require the Accounting Office to trace out the records pertaining to the CIP accounts without the supporting schedules and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in securing the copies of accomplishment report on the implemented projects on previous years;
2) Prepare and maintain CIPLC for each on-going infrastructure projects; and
3) Require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer whose powers and duties requires her to take custody of and accountable for all properties real or personal owned by the Agency, to secure the copy of the Monthly Status Report of Projects Undertaken and validate the existence of the ongoing infrastructure projects to attest to the correctness of the accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents of all accumulated accounts payable, as reflected in the Schedule of Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2018, to back up the authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government. We suggested that in-depth evaluation be undertaken to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables both for the current and prior years ledgers/files to determine which are valid claims or fictitious accounts. Moreover, for those established transactions with no valid claimants and dormant accounts, the Office of the Municipal Accountant must take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the respective amount payable to the 22 barangays of the Municipal Government as well as to the Province of Masbate as mandated under the existing regulations.

Further, require the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly on the guidelines relative to the prompt release of the barangay shares from realty taxes as stated under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code and shares from CTCs collections in the ensuing years.

We recommended that Management design and implement real property tax generation program which must include tax mapping, complete and updated taxpayers records and strategies to improve tax collection efficiency especially on delinquent account.  Send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and avail the aforementioned legal remedies.  Moreover, inculcate to taxpayers, thru information dissemination, that taxes collected were applied to address the most urgent needs of the general public.

We recommended the submission of the foregoing lacking supporting documents.  Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant or the accounting personnel to have a thorough review on the completeness of the supporting documents to be attached to the claim for the payment of procurement of infrastructure projects and impose strictly the proper documentation before processing. Further, see to it that the prescribed period on the submission of documents for contract review of the infrastructure projects and disbursement accounts are being followed.

We recommended to require the Officials concerned to prepare/maintain the details of the implemented projects to be reflected in the Registry of Appropriations and SAOB and Quarterly Report on PPAs to facilitate the monitoring of the status of fund and accomplishments of projects.  Henceforth, require prior approval/or authorization by the Sanggunian before the Local Chief Executive enter into contract/incur expenses in behalf of the LGU for appropriated fund that describe the project in generic terms under lump sum appropriation.

We recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM.  Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back up in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash and liability accounts in the financial statements; and
The Accounting Office coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances QRF, LDRRM-MOOE and LDRRMF-CO for the current year and previous years.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO, in close coordination with the MDRRMC, to prepare a well-planned/studied LDRRMFIP which must also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years which must be implemented as planned in order to optimize utilization and bring the desired benefits to its constituents and to avoid future reversion of the fund to the General fund.  Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and RA 10121 is required.
