observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System promptly prepare and submit the agency’s   GAD Plan and  Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office(PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the city is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.
Furthermore, the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report be also submitted to the DILG for the corresponding assessment of the reported PPAs undertaken and for its consolidation to be reported to the Office of the PCW.

We recommended that the Management direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and the LDRRM Officer to submit a copy therefore to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was able to partially submit the DRRMF monthly report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and MunicipalAccountant to render their accounts and submit the same for audit in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and Section 59, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance as a result of post-audit.

We recommended the a) Municipal Accounting Office to strictly observe the proper accounting of 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund as set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, particularly the maintenance of Separate Subsidiary Ledgers and Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for QRF, MOOE & CO; and the b) LDRRMO to properly and efficiently utilize the 5% LDRRMF strictly for calamity-related activities, supported with proper documentation so as to accomplish all planned projects and activities. The LDRRMO is also encouraged to review the formulation and mainstreaming of the LDRRM Plan and Fund Investment Plan in order to avoid unnecessary projects that defeat the objectives of RA 10121 and DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.reason: Maintenance of separate Subsidiary Ledgers and RAAO for QRF, MOOE & CO is yet to be implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to terminate the services of the private lawyer and instead avail the services of the provincial legal officer for any legal advice and opinion, for drafting and making of ordinary contracts, and other legal services which the latter is not disqualified to handle pursuant to Paragraph 3(1) Section 481 of RA 7160. The LGU should hire only the services of a private lawyer only in exceptional cases and must comply with the requirements laid down in COA Circular 98-002, that prior written conformity and acquiescence of the Office of the Solicitor General and written concurrence of COA must be secured first before hiring of private lawyer.

We therefore recommended the Management to strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the Departmentof Budget and Management implementing the certain provisions of RA 7160. Payment of honoraria to officials of COMELEC and BIR may be disallowed in audit for lack of legal basis.

We recommended that Management should comply strictly with the provisions provided by the aforementioned laws and secure the required Environmental Compliance Certificate to avoid further penal and administrative liabilities.

We recommended that the management, technical working group and GAD focal person should: a) closely coordinate with the heads of office on the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities based on approved plans and budgets b) strictly comply with the submission of the Accomplishment Report as mandated by COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 and c) implement programs, projects and activities only for which the GAD funds have been appropriated.

observations.managementAction: No appropriate action taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to closely monitor and evaluate the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure timely implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs, projects and activities.

Likewise, the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP as provided in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the receivable account from the Barangays and demand the submission of the liquidation reports as basis for closing the receivable account. Barangays Bookkeepers should also be advised to record the Financial Assistance as a trust liability in the barangay books.

We recommended that management direct the GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office, to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards and to conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of all PPE accounts. The differences between the physical count and the balances per books must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. Assign additional personnel to conduct physical count of all PPE accounts.

Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Market Administrator, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials for the setting up of special accounts in the general fund books in accordance with Section 313 of RA 7160.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit separate Subsidiary Ledgers and separate financial reports for special accounts as embodied under Section 105 and 110 on the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 on the timely submission and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies (NGAs).  The Municipal Accountant is advised to collate records to ensure that all unutilized balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC Secretariat, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on submission of copies of perfected contracts within five (5) days after their execution. Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 of Presidential Decree No.1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of Republic Act No. 6713.

3.6	We recommended that Management through the Local School Board, to include programs, projects and activities for Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) in the Annual Budget of Special Education Fund in adherence to Section 7(b) of Republic Act No. 10410.

4.6 We recommended that Management through the LCPC align the programs and activities of LCPC Plan set under in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.  If warranted, make a periodic review and revision of their programs and activities of LCPCP in order to address the present local needs and concerns involving children justice and welfare.

5.6	We recommended that Management closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the project in accordance with contract plans and specifications.  Projects signboard must be installed at project location in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.  Close coordination with the DILG (MLGOO) must be taken to ensure compliance with the policy and guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-03.
5.7	Initiate appropriate actions in case of delay/negative slippage in the implementation of the project including the imposition of liquidated damages, if warranted.

6.5	We recommended that Management fast tract the completion of all on-going and not implemented projects funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.

7.6	We recommended that Management, through the MDRRM Office formulate a Comprehensive and Integrated MDRRM Plan aligned with the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan 2011-2028 (NDRRMP).The MDRRM Office is advised to closely monitor the activities in the MDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risk and enhancing disaster capabilities at LGU level.

8.7	We recommended that Management through the MSWMB, fully execute the programs and activities contained in the 10 Year MESWMP, undertake self-assessment report and make corrective action plan to address the present issues and concerns on ecological environment.

9.6	We recommended that Management through the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG to comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 in the implementation of GPB.  Periodic review and corrections should be made on GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women including other gender related issues.

10.6	We recommended that Management execute Contract of Lessee with all existing lessees occupying the market stalls in consonance with Municipal Market Code.  Advertise the vacant stalls, accept new lessees, and continuously monitor the payment of rental fees including the enforcement of penalties/surcharge on late payment.  Additional funds be also provided for repairs and maintenance of the establishment to improve its appearance and attract potential customers and prospective lessees.

11.6	We recommended that Management formulate a Policy Guidelines in hiring  contractual/job order employees, taking into consideration the necessity as to the reasonable number of contractual/job order employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and expenses for wages.
11.7	Ensure that the policy guidelines formulated does not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality and will promote optimum utilization of financial resources.

12.6 We recommended that Management require the Liquidating officer to turnover collections to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance to the provisions of the above mentioned regulations to strengthen the control on handling and deposit of collections.

13.6	We recommended that the Local Treasurer adopt and strictly implement the procedures pertaining to issuance and monitoring of accountable forms as prescribed in Section 59, Chapter 2, Book 2 of Local Treasury Operations Manual of Bureau of Local Government Finance dated October 1, 2007.

We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Accountant to record the balance of previous year’s loan granted to farmer beneficiaries amounting to P438, 439.00 as Loans Receivable-Others to monitor the collection thereof.

b.	Management install effective and efficient control procedures in the identification of active farmer borrowers.  Closely monitor the utilization of the loans to ensure its utilization sustainability and the effectiveness of the programs as perceived.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the audit disallowances totaling to P915, 600.00 as receivable in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Account maintain detailed subsidiary ledgers as a monitoring tool for the collections of receivables and settlement of claims of suppliers/creditors.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended management direct concerned officials/employees to record purchases of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method and comply with the provisions as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs and/or the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).  Closely monitor the undistributed/unreleased Hybrid rice seeds and fertilizers to avoid possible loss of government funds.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to submit immediately the subject reports. Thereafter, instruct the accountable officers, City Accountant, City Treasurer, City budget Officer and all concerned to submit promptly or timely the disbursement and collection reports together with the paid vouchers, payrolls and official receipts; JEVs, and other financial reports within the reglementary period set by the regulation. Suspend the payment of salaries until they should have complied with the requirements.

We recommended that management instruct the concerned Accountable Officer (AO) to strictly comply with the provisions under paragraph 5.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure that proper accounting of the cash advances granted to her were done by the Accounting upon the receipt of the liquidation documents submitted to them.

We recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Cashier/Disbursing Officers and the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the above-cited instructions on the preparation and accomplishment of the Report of Checks Issued.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board should limit the utilization of SEF to the priority programs and projects pursuant Sec. 272 of RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A series of 2000 and 01-B, series of 2001 to ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of resources meant for the development of the country’s education system. Likewise, implement capital outlay projects intended for the construction/repair/maintenance of school buildings and facilities in order to attain the priority objectives of the SEF.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and DRRM Officer to refrain from using the LDRRMF on expenses which are not related to and does not support the disaster risk management activities. The Municipal Officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 21 of RA 10121.

We recommended that the management require all collecting officers to remit on time and intact their collections, otherwise, cash shortages that maybe incurred in the future shall be dealt with more strictly.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor, as the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) require the BAC Secretariat to post all the Notices of Awards and Notices to Proceed, together with the approved contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects and goods and services in the PhilGEPS, within the prescribed period pursuant to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that management require the contractor to give a valid and reasonable explanation for the delayed completion of the project. Management should strictly monitor the accomplishment of all projects to avoid delay of implementation.

We also required management to submit the documents to support the grant of 120 calendar days of time extension to the contractor for further review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management should stop granting cash incentive awards to LGU employees and elective officials that is contrary to CSC PRAISE and Articles 77 and 170(c) of the IRR of RA 7160 in payment of compensation and benefits.

We recommended that the Management rcquirc the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately and in full all undeposited collections as of December 31,2016 with the authorized depository bank. Require the collectors to remit all their collections daily to avoid incurrence of shortages and/or possible misuse of public funds. Strict adherence to the abovementioned rules and regulations relative to the collections and deposits is required.

observations.recommendation: 1.8 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to conduct physical inventory of the properties of the municipality and to prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records.  Submit the same to the auditor concerned pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 122 of P. D. 1445, for proper review and verification.
2.9	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

4.11	We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant record the balance of previous year’s loan granted to farmer beneficiaries amounting to P438, 439.00 as Loans Receivable-Others to monitor the collection thereof.
Management install effective and efficient control procedures in the identification of active farmer borrowers.  Closely monitor the utilization of the loans to ensure sustainability and the effectiveness of the programs as perceived.
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agricultural Office reconcile the recorded difference in outstanding loans receivable on the loans granted to farmer beneficiaries amounting to P847, 417.00.
Management strengthen the existing system of control on the delivery, receipt and issuance of procured agricultural supplies for loans to qualified farmer beneficiaries to ensure maximum and efficient utilization of government resources and safeguarded from possible loss through theft or misappropriation.

5.4	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the audit disallowances totaling to P915, 600.00 as receivable in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
6.8	We recommended that the Municipal Account maintain detailed subsidiary ledgers as a monitoring tool for the collections of receivables and settlement of claims of suppliers/creditors.

7.4	We recommended management direct concerned officials/employees to record purchases of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method and comply with the provisions as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs and/or the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).  Closely monitor the undistributed/unreleased Hybrid rice seeds and fertilizers to avoid possible loss of government funds.

9.11	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to closely monitor and evaluate the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure timely implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs, projects and activities.
9.12	Likewise, the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP as provided in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.

We recommend	ed that management require the concerned officials to submit immediately the subject reports. Thereafter, instruct the accountable officers, City Accountant, City Treasurer, City budget Officer and all concerned to submit promptly or timely the disbursement and collection reports together with the paid vouchers, payrolls and official receipts; JEVs, and other financial reports within the reglementary period set by the regulation. Suspend the payment of salaries until they should have complied with the requirements.

We recommended that management instruct the concerned Accountable Officer (AO) to strictly comply with the provisions under paragraph 5.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure that proper accounting of the cash advances granted to her were done by the Accounting upon the receipt of the liquidation documents submitted to them.
We also recommended that the date of liquidation should be reflected in the cashbook to ascertain whether liquidations were made within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Cashier/Disbursing Officers and the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the above-cited instructions on the preparation and accomplishment of the Report of Checks Issued.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board should limit the utilization of SEF to the priority programs and projects pursuant Sec. 272 of RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A series of 2000 and 01-B, series of 2001 to ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of resources meant for the development of the country’s education system. Likewise, implement capital outlay projects intended for the construction/repair/maintenance of school buildings and facilities in order to attain the priority objectives of the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the receivable account from the Barangays and demand the submission of the liquidation reports as basis for closing the receivable account. Barangays Bookkeepers should also be advised to record the Financial Assistance as a trust liability in the barangay books.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and DRRM Officer to refrain from using the LDRRMF on expenses which are not related to and does not support the disaster risk management activities. The Municipal Officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 21 of RA 10121.

We recommended that the management require all collecting officers to remit on time and intact their collections, otherwise, cash shortages that maybe incurred in the future shall be dealt with more strictly.

We recommended that management direct the GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office, to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards and to conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of all PPE accounts. The differences between the physical count and the balances per books must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. Assign additional personnel to conduct physical count of all PPE accounts.

Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Market Administrator, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials for the setting up of special accounts in the general fund books in accordance with Section 313 of RA 7160.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit separate Subsidiary Ledgers and separate financial reports for special accounts as embodied under Section 105 and 110 on the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor, as the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) require the BAC Secretariat to post all the Notices of Awards and Notices to Proceed, together with the approved contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects and goods and services in the PhilGEPS, within the prescribed period pursuant to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that management require the contractor to give a valid and reasonable explanation for the delayed completion of the project. Management should strictly monitor the accomplishment of all projects to avoid delay of implementation.
We also required management to submit the documents to support the grant of 120 calendar days of time extension to the contractor for further review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management should stop granting cash incentive awards to LGU employees and elective officials that is contrary to CSC PRAISE and Articles 77 and 170(c) of the IRR of RA 7160 in payment of compensation and benefits.

One of the audit objectives in the verification of the PPE accounts is to ascertain
whether all PPE are stated at cost less accumulated depreciation and to determine
whether depreciation| of these assets had been properly and accurately computed and
provided.

Paragraph 59 to 78 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS)

depreciable amount, Tesidual value and useful life of PPE.

the financial statements could not be ascertained.

documents. 1
